text: Video-assisted thoracic surgery is an exciting surgical technique to diagnose and treat problems in the chest.
In treating lung cancer, it can be used to remove masses close to the outside edges of the lung and to test them for cancer.
Eastern Time, you can see a video-assisted thoracic surgery for yourself in a premiere webcast presented by Beth Israel Medical Center in collaboration with the Continuum Cancer Centers of New York.
and the advantages of advanced video technology over traditional open procedures.
You'll also hear from a real patient who has undergone the procedure at Beth Israel and about her experience.
